demographics.living_situation: lives at home with parents who provide full-time care

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help us — our 18-year-old son has Smith-Magenis syndrome and he's been awake for over 24 hours. Now he's having a massive meltdown at 3am, smashing his head against the walls, and he's shoved a plastic bead deep into his ear. We can't calm him down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He's completely out of control and hurting himself, and there's a bead stuck in his ear — we couldn't wait until morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, after he had been awake for more than 24 hours
symptom_narrative.character: an explosive tantrum with screaming, spasmodic self-hugging, then repeated head-banging against the walls; during it he forced a small plastic bead deep into his right ear
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crisis we've had in a long time — we cannot contain him
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his head from the banging, and his right ear where the bead is
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: always tied to his reversed sleep pattern — worse when he hasn't slept
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; his night-time melatonin did not work today
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: exhaustion and being awake all night
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: his voice is hoarse from screaming and there is bruising on his forehead from the head-banging
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he gets these crises when his sleep gets disrupted, but tonight is severe
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: needs constant supervision to stay safe; depends entirely on his routines

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Smith-Magenis syndrome, a genetic condition diagnosed when he was young
past_medical_history.how_managed: strict daily routines, melatonin at night, and behavioural support

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: moderate intellectual disability; he is non-verbal when he is in crisis
a badly reversed sleep-wake pattern — he is often awake all night
past_medical_history.how_managed: melatonin and a rigid bedtime routine

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he does not seem to feel pain the way we do and does not protect himself when hurt

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Lives at home with parents who provide 24-hour care; the house is modified with padded corners for safety; the parents are severely sleep-deprived from managing his night-time behaviour.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Frightened parents reporting on behalf of a son who cannot speak for himself during the crisis.

objective_data_offered.value: he is highly agitated, pacing, and repeatedly striking his head against the wall while screaming hoarsely
superficial bruising visible on his forehead
he has pushed a small hard plastic bead deep into his right ear and we can no longer see it
he has been awake for over 24 hours and did not sleep despite his melatonin
he does not guard or protect the bruised area and does not seem to feel the pain
there is no blood or fluid coming from his ear that we can see, and he does not feel hot or feverish
